MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rowena Navia appeared by zoom for David Keeler (administrator).
Tenesha Jackson appeared by zoom for Herbert Albert (former administrator).

The court noted that the probate examiner's notes from 2023 have not been addressed. Mrs. Navia stated that there were a lot of misunderstandings regarding the administration of the estate due to Ms. Friedeberg's passing, that she is communicating with Mrs. Jackson, that she was able to get a declaration regarding Mrs. Jackson's services and that she has been under the weather and needs time to review the amended petition. Mrs. Jackson stated that she is okay with a continuance and that she understands the situation with Ms. Friedeberg. The First and Final Account and Report, etc. is continued to September 11, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Jaime. Not reported.

Off Calendar Mar-30-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg, via zoom, for David Keeler, Administrator.

The court waives notice defects pursuant to recent filings made by the Petitioner. The sale was confirmed at $610,000 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $620,000 is required.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Kahler. Not reported.

Ex Parte Petition For Authority To Enter Into An Exclusive Listing Agreement For Sale Of Real Property; And For Authority To Dispose Of Tangible Personal Property In The House By Sale Or Liquidation
Off Calendar Jan-31-2022 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-20-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Schultz, Esq., Creditor/Petitioner in pro per (appearing by video conference); Trisha Friedeberg, Esq., counsel for and with David Keeler, proposed Administrator (appearing separately by video conference); Tenesha Jackson, Esq., counsel for and with Herbert Albert II, suspended Administrator (Ms. Jackson appearing by video conference, Mr. Albert present in the Courtroom)

Court and counsel review the status of the physical estate and its financial status, including the condition of the real estate and the pending tax sale of the home. After discussion, all parties concede that sale of the property is the only viable option at this time given Mr. Albert's opposition to securing a loan on the property.

Counsel advise the Court that at this time, there is no personal representative of the estate as Mr. Albert was suspended as administrator and Mr. Keeler's letters of special administration have expired. At the Court's query, Mr. Albert declines to resign as administrator if Mr. Keeler is to be the successor administrator. Court briefly recesses.

Upon the Court's return, the Court orders Herbert Albert II removed as administrator of the Estate of Herbert Albert Sr. and grants the petition appointing David Keeler the Administrator of the Estate of Herbert Albert Sr. with limited authority under the Independent Administration of Estates Act. Bond is waived. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for hearing on January 9, 2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. Isidoor Bornstein is appointed Probate Referee.

The Court orders the current accounting petition off calendar and orders Mr. Albert II to file an accounting for the pendency of his administration. Ms. Jackson advises that the estate was entirely insolvent during Mr. Albert's time as personal representative and received no funds at all. The Court will accept a declaration from Ms. Jackson to that effect in lieu of the accounting.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Dillingham. Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-06-2021 Continued To Dec-13-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Andrew Schultz, via ZOOM, in propria persona, Petitioner; Trisha Friedeberg, via ZOOM, for David Keeler, via ZOOM, proposed Administrator; Tenesha Jackson, via ZOOM, for Herbert Albert, III, in person, suspended Administrator.

Mr. Schultz provides oral testimony regarding the history of this matter and the several, current, issues with the administration of the estate including, but not limited to, that the property taxes have not been paid on the real property and that he has been advised by the San Francisco tax collector that it plans to sell the home via auction in Spring of 2022 to pay for the outstanding tax bill. Mr. Schultz also advises that there has been a delay in receiving signed promissory notes from Mr. Albert, III. Ms. Jackson provides testimony regarding the history of her involvement in this matter and advises that her client has signed and notarized the documents that Mr. Schultz is waiting for and that she is waiting for her client to return them to her. Ms. Jackson also reports that her client wants to pay all outstanding debts via a loan against the property but that he is unable to obtain one until the deed is transferred to his name. Ms. Friedeberg notes her concerns with the history of administration of this matter and asks that her client be appointed so that he can begin administration. The court advises the parties that it intends to continue this matter to be heard with the pending accounting petition. The court then reminds Mr. Albert, III, of the importance of him forwarding the papers to Mr. Schultz prior to the next calling of this matter.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to Dec-13-2021 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Charlene P. Kiesselbach. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

EX PARTE PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Proof Of Home Insurance Coverage
Off Calendar Aug-02-2021 Continued To Sep-20-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Aug-02-2021 Continued To Sep-20-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Tenesha Jackson, for Herber Albert III, in person, Executor; Trisha Friedeberg, via ZOOM, in propria persona, Creditor; Avid Alavi, via ZOOM, specially appearing for Andrew Schultz, Objector.

The court begins by pointing to Mr. Albert that proof of insurance has not been filed, as requested at the previous calling of this matter. Mr. Albert reports that he has obtained insurance coverage from Farmers Insurance company. The court reminds Mr. Albert that he had previously stated that he had existing home insurance coverage via AAA. Mr. Albert reports that AAA cancelled the policy once they received information that the home is unoccupied and then went to Farmers to obtain a policy. The court then asks Mr. Albert for proof of insurance. Mr. Albert advises that he has the policy number and other related information, but the court reminds Mr. Albert that the information he is willing to share must be formally filed with the court and shared with the other parties in this matter. Ms. Alavi reports that she would like to see proof of the insurance. Ms. Alavi also reports that she believes that a special administrator should be appointed in order to help the estate keep the home as she has seen other cases where the estate loses the property because of debts not being paid. Ms. Friedeberg agrees with Ms. Alavi's suggestion to appoint a special administrator to set deadlines. The court then hears from Mr. Albert regarding his efforts up to this point as Executor. The court reminds Mr. Albert of some of his responsibilities as Executor which up to this point, he has failed to meet. Ms. Jackson reports that regrettably, Mr. Albert's refusal to communicate with her has caused the current issues with this matter. The court strongly urges Mr. Albert to contact Ms. Jackson and to be more responsive to her. The court advises that it is suspending Mr.

Albert as Personal Representative and advises that Mr. Schultz should file a petition to appoint a special administrator. Ms. Alavi confirms that she will discuss the preceding with Mr. Schultz. The court then reminds Mr. Albert to share the proof of insurance to the parties in this matter and to formally file it with the court. Ms. Jackson advises that she is willing to assist Mr. Albert with the preceding.

The Status on Close of Estate and Status on Filing of Proof of Home Insurance Coverage are continued to September 20, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: C. Andam. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jul-19-2021 Continued To Aug-02-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Tenesha Jackson, via ZOOM, for Herbert Jackson II, in court, Personal Representative; Trisha Friedeberg, via ZOOM, for David Keeler, Requestor; Andrew Schultz, via ZOOM, for Witherspoon and Siracusa, Creditor.

Ms. Jackson begins by advising the court that she has sent her client an accounting and promissory notes for him to review, sign, and return to her but that she has not received a reply from Mr. Albert III. The court summarizes the previous calling of this matter and advises Mr. Jackson that it appears that there has not been much work done to advance the case since then. Mr. Albert III reports that he no longer wishes for Ms. Jackson to represent him which is why he has not replied to her. Mr. Albert III advises that he is hoping to obtain a loan to assist him with paying with the outstanding payments. Mr. Schultz advises that he has done some research and it appears as though the property taxes have not been paid in about eight (8) years. Mr. Schultz proposes suspending Mr. Albert III and appointing a professional fiduciary. Ms. Friedeberg offers her oral testimony and agrees with Mr. Schultz. There is also discussion of whether the real property is currently covered by insurance. Mr. Albert III reports that the property is currently insured by AAA. The court advises Mr. Albert III that proof of insurance will need to be submitted to the court prior to the next calling of this matter. The court also advises the parties that the court will consider issuing an Order to Show Cause regarding Removal or Suspension of Administrator.

The Status on close of Estate is continued to August 2, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204. A Status on Proof of Home Insurance Coverage is set for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: B. Quesada. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar May-17-2021 Continued To Jul-19-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Tenesha Jackson, via ZOOM, counsel for Herbert Albert II, personal appearance, Administrator; Trisha Friedeberg, via ZOOM, counsel for David Keeler, Creditor; Andrew Schultz, for Witherspoon & Siracusa, Creditor.

Court and counsel begin by discussing the status of the close of the estate. Ms. Jackson reports that she has prepared papers and is waiting for Mr. Albert to return them to her for finalizing. Mr. Albert confirms receipt of the papers but reports that he is not in agreement with some of the terms included within the document and has asked Ms. Jackson to remove the terms. Court and counsel then discuss the several outstanding payments Mr. Albert owes and report that the creditors have all been working with Mr. Albert to come to terms with regard to the owed. Mr. Albert then reports that work had slowed due to the pandemic but lately, there has been an increase in work. The parties confirm that the only asset remaining is the real property. Court then asks whether it might be time to consider selling the home in order to pay the creditors. Mr. Albert reports that he does not wish to sell the property. Ms. Jackson then reports that the property is currently vacant and requires work and is currently not in condition to be rented. Mr. Albert believes that his economic position will improve soon and requests additional time.

After additional discussion, the Status on Close of Estate is continued to July 19, 2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Dec-15-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Tenesha Jackson appeared by zoom for and with Herbert Albert in court.
Rowena Navia specially appearing for Trisha Friedeberg by zoom on behalf of David Keeler (Creditor).
Andrew Schultz appeared by zoom for Witherspoon & Siracusa (Creditor).

Ms. Jackson stated that she was able to meet and confer with Mr. Schultz and Ms. Friedeberg, that they reached an agreement with regard to how to make payment, that she is in the process of receiving updated promissory notes, and requested a continuance to get documents signed and file a petition for final distribution on waiver of account. Mr. Schultz stated that they all talked, that they both provided draft promissory notes, and that six months is enough time to get the documents signed and for Ms. Jackson to file a petition for final distribution. The Petition for Order Approving Report on Status of Administration is under submission. Status on close of estate is set for May 17, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Oct-28-2020 Continued To Nov-16-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Tenesha Jackson appeared by zoom for and with Herbert Albert, Jr. (in court).
Andrew Schultz appeared by zoom for Witherspoon & Siracusa (Creditor).
Trisha Friedeberg appeared by zoom for Kato, Feder & Suzuki (Creditor) and for David Keeler (Creditor).

Ms. Jackson stated that her Client was able to get a pre-approval letter for a $400,000.00 loan, Mr. Albert provided a document from US Bank dated October 11, 2020 titled "Purchase Ready Buyer Certificate" which shows prequalification for a 30-year fixed loan with a sales price of $400,000.00 and a loan amount of $386,000.00 effective for 90-days. The Court noted that this is the first piece of concrete evidence Mr. Albert has provided with respect to obtaining a loan. Ms. Jackson stated that the amount of $386,000.00 is more than sufficient to cover the outstanding fees, asked the Court to give her Client time to finalize the loan process and stated that she will not be available after November 24th due to a medical issue. Ms. Friedeberg stated that it sounds like they are ready to move forward and that they need to talk about logistics. Mr. Schultz stated that he agrees this is the first piece of evidence Mr. Albert has provided and he is happy it is moving forward, that it is not clear that this is sufficient given the condition of the property, that the loan needs to be specific to this property and that an appraisal may be required before the loan is approved. Ms. Jackson stated that the bank will not move forward with the loan without the property being in Mr. Albert's name and that it is being considered a refinance. Mr. Schultz stated that he understands that, and he believes the Creditors are willing to pull back the liens, but they need something in writing and that Medi-Cal needs to be involved. Ms. Jackson stated that Medi-Cal agrees to take a lien against the property, or they are willing to get monthly payments, or the Client can pay it all off. The Petition for Order Approving Report on Status of Administration is continued to November 16, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Examiner: Helen Trowbridge. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Sep-30-2020 Off Calendar

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Sep-30-2020 Continued To Oct-28-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Tenesha Jackson appeared by zoom for and with Herbert Albert, Jr. (in court).
Andrew Schultz appeared by zoom for Witherspoon & Siracusa (Creditor).
Rowena Navia by zoom specially appearing for Trisha Friedeberg on behalf of David Keeler (Creditor).
Janet Boessenecker appeared by zoom for San Francisco Public Administrator.

The Court noted that the Creditors are being patient to see if Mr. Albert was able to obtain a loan. Mr. Albert stated that when he spoke with the Credit Union the original way to go was to put the house in his name which was over four years ago, that when he went back to the Credit Union it triggered them to do something different and he got the sense that the Credit Union felt he was trying to do something shady, and they haven't talked to him. The Court stated that perhaps Mr. Albert is not in a position to make this happen. Ms. Jackson stated that this is a process Mr. Albert has done on his own without assistance by her office, that what they need is a pre-qualifying letter, that she thinks it was not explained properly and that it is an issue of a snowball of mixed information. Ms. Jackson also stated that she explained to her Client the need to keep open communication, asked if the Court would allow her to talk with the bank on behalf of Mr. Albert, stated that they will either get a loan or sell, and that she has a medical issue and won't be available until the first week of February. The Court stated that it was prepared to remove Mr. Albert, that it is concerned about the condition of the property and that it doesn't want Mr. Albert to be left with nothing. The Court also stated that Mr. Albert is at the fork in the road, that he either gets financing, or needs to sell the property and that Mr. Albert can't keep it going on forever. Mr. Schultz stated that they all want Mr. Albert to keep the house if he can, that Mr. Albert was given time in January and then given 60-days in March, and provided history. Ms. Navia stated that 30-days is okay and that she would like to give Mr. Albert a chance to see if this can go forward. Ms. Boessencker stated that if the Administrator is removed, it is her understanding that Mr. Keeler is willing to act, that it would be the Public Administrator's preference, that the Public Administrator was Mr. Albert, Sr.'s conservator and that there was some distress with that. The Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration is continued to October 28, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for obtaining approval of a loan and clearing title. The Court encouraged Mr. Albert to work with Ms. Jackson. The order to appear is off calendar.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Helen Trowbridge. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Sep-16-2020 Continued To Sep-30-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Tenesha Jackson appeared by zoom for Herbert Albert II.
Andrew Schultz appeared by zoom for Witherspoon & Siracusa (Creditor).
Trisha Friedeberg appeared by zoom for David Keeler (Creditor) and Kato, Feder & Suzuki (Creditor).

The Court noted that Mr. Albert is not present and asked for status of the loan. Ms. Jackson stated that she does not have a definitive answer and that the last she heard a couple weeks ago was that Mr. Albert's loan/pre-approval was in process. The Court expressed concern stating that Mr. Albert has been trying to get a loan for quite some time. Mr. Schultz stated that when he was Court Appointed Counsel the issue of the house was at central, that he would like Mr. Albert to have every opportunity to purchase the house, that the Decedent has been dead for four years and that this case has been on for three years. Ms. Friedeberg stated they have all tried to provide as much leave way as possible to allow Mr. Albert to keep the property in the family, that this is nothing new and that she feels it is appropriate for the Court to set a deadline by which the property is to be put up for sale. The Court stated that it is concerned about dissipation and raised the issue of whether it should remove Mr. Albert as the Administrator and appoint the Public Administrator. The Petition for Order Approving Report on Status of Administration is continued to September 30, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. An order to appear will be issued to the Administrator Herbert Albert to appear on September 30, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to bring an update on the loan. if a loan is not tangible and is not a practical reality, the Court intends either removal of the Administrator, and setting a schedule for sale of the property. Ms. Jackson is to tell Mr. Albert that the Court is very disappointed that he is not here today.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Court Examiner: Helen Trowbridge. Deputy: L. Maloney. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jul-08-2020 Continued To Sep-16-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Tenesha Jackson for and with Herbert Albert, Jr. (Petitioner).
Andrew Schultz appeared by telephone for Witherspoon & Siracusa (Creditor).
Trisha Friedeberg appeared by telephone for and with David Keeler (Former Trustee) by telephone.

The Court questions whether a loan has been obtained and whether the Creditors have been paid. Ms. Jackson stated that her Client looked into acquiring an administrative loan, but the payments would be too expensive, that they have spoken with the Creditors, and that the Creditors all agree with Mr. Albert taking title of the property and obtaining a conventional loan. Ms. Jackson also stated that they requested 120-days from the date of transfer of title to pay the debts, that her Client went to a credit union and did qualify for a loan in an amount to cover the amounts owed, and that they are looking at a loan in the amount of $400,000.00 to pay all of the outstanding debts. Mr. Schultz stated that he represented the Conservatee, that his Client objected to the sale of the house and that the Conservatee wanted Mr. Albert, Jr. to have the property. Mr. Schultz also stated that he agrees in principal and to getting a note and deed of trust from Mr. Albert, and that he requested back in January to having something indicating that Medi-Cal would accept this plan and that Mr. Albert get a pre-approval letter. In addition, Mr. Schultz stated that he is a little worried about the loan to value ratio and the condition of the house, and whether a lender would be willing to provide a loan. Furthermore, Mr. Schultz suggested continuing the matter for 60-days for Mr. Albert to get the loan and see if it is feasible, and to see if all of the parties can agree on terms and notes. Ms. Friedeberg stated that she agrees with Mr. Schultz and that they need something in writing. The Court expressed concern about this plan stating that it transfers the property outside of the estate and that there would be no motivation to settle the estate. The Petition for Order Approving Report on Status of Administration is continued to September 16, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court encourages Mr. Albert to work the lien holders and to see if everyone is on board. An agreement is to be filed by September 9, 2020.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Mar-18-2020 Continued To Apr-15-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jan-13-2020 Continued To Mar-18-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Tenesha Jackson for and with Herbert Albert.
Andrew Schultz Court appointed Counsel for the Decedent when he was a Conservatee now a Creditor.
Trisha Friedeberg (Creditor) in Propria Persona.

Ms. Jackson requested a 60-day continuance stating that her Client was in the process of getting a personal loan, and that her Client was trying to avoid the cost of a fiduciary loan, but a fiduciary loan appears to be the only way to get a loan. Mr. Schultz stated that he tried to reach Michael Albert, that there was a mix up with the paper work and that Michael is not a Son of the Decedent. The Court stated that it is not happy with Mr. Albert's efforts, that fees are due and have been due for a long time, that the case has been lagging and that nothing seems to be happening. The Court also stated that it is inclined to remove Mr. Albert and appoint Mr. Keeler, and noted that there is also a Medi-Cal lien. Ms. Jackson stated that her Client is the sole heir, that all of the Creditor's fees are secured by the real property and that the only person to be harmed is her Client. The Court stated that it does not know what steps Mr. Albert has taken. Ms. Jackson stated that Mr. Albert has only gone to the Police Credit Union, but Mr. Schultz has provided Mr. Albert with names of other banks/lenders. The Court stated that the matter will be continued for two months for an order to show cause as to why Mr. Albert should not be removed. Ms. Friedeberg stated that she is fine with 60-days and that she will check with Mr. Keeler. The Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration is continued to March 18, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Cecilia P. Castellanos. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

Tenesha Jackson for and with Herbert Albert II.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jul-29-2019 Continued To Sep-09-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Tenesha Jackson for and with Herbert Albert (Petitioner).
Trisha Friedeberg for herself and David Keeler as Creditors.
Andrew Schultz on behalf of Witherspoon & Siracusa (Creditor).

The Court stated that there is a notice issue and that even though the parties that are present can waive notice, there is no one here from the Department of Health Care Services ("DHCS"). Ms. Jackson stated that it was an oversight on her part, that she has spoken with a Representative from DHCS, that he did not express objection and that her Client met with a Lender that is willing to loan money to him personally, but not as an Administrator. Ms. Jackson also stated that the only problem is that the title of the property is not in her Client's name, that the plan is to see if the Creditor's can agree to allow distribution subject to the existing liens, and that if they don't agree, they will have to sell the house. The Court informed Ms. Jackson that it wants to see a current title report by August 19, 2019. Mr. Schultz clarified that he is not objecting to the sale of the house, and stated that his Client wanted his Son to keep the house, but understood that it may not be possible. The Petition for Order Approving Report on Status of Administration is continued to September 9, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Tenesha Jackson for and with Herbert Albert.
Lawrence Siracusa (Creditor) in Propria Persona.

Ms. Jackson stated that there was miscommunication that lead to a lapse in communication, that it has been rectified, that she believes a status report is the better form because there is no cash and requested 120-days to file the report. The Court questions why it will take so long to file the report and informed Ms. Jackson that she needs to file an inventory and appraisal for the real property. Ms. Jackson stated that she can file the inventory in 30-days. Status on close of estate is continued to May 1, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of both said documents.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jan-23-2019 Continued To Feb-20-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Herbert Albert in Propria Persona.

Mr. Albert stated that he was told by his Attorney that he was supposed be here today, that he is having problems and he does not know where to turn, that he has not been getting receipts and that things are not getting done. The Court informed Mr. Albert that he needs to speak with his Attorney and that if he not satisfied with the services he is getting, he can hire another Attorney. An Order to Appear will be issued the Attorney to appear on February 20, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why he failed to appear, and why a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration has not been filed. Status on close of estate is also continued to that date. Mr. Albert is also ordered to be present.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
